title: Shasa
content:

{{Quote|I must apologize for doubting you. The Sith are truly as evil as you have claimed. […] We thank you, human, for showing us the truth. You have saved us from a terrible mistake. Quickly, my friends—we can stay here no longer. We must flee this foul embassy and warn our people against the plot to corrupt the Manaan youth.|translated=1|Shasa, to Revan|<ref name="KotOR game" />}}
'''Shasa''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who lived during the [[Jedi Civil War]], a conflict initiated by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], [[Darth Malak]]. In the war's [[3956 BBY|final year]], Shasa and other Selkath youth were lured into the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire's]] [[Sith Embassy|Embassy]] on the Selkath [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]] by an [[Iridonian (species)|Iridonian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]]. Shasa and her friends joined the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], who brainwashed them using [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]]. The Selkath [[Sith training|learned]] the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] under the Embassy's [[Unidentified Dark Jedi Master (Manaan)|Dark Jedi Master]].

Shasa encountered the former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Revan, who had been [[Capture of Darth Revan|captured]] and [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], during his [[Raid on the Sith Embassy|raid]] on the Sith Embassy. Revan, who infiltrated the Embassy on behalf of Shasa's [[Parent/Legends|father]] [[Shaelas]], tried to convince Shasa and the other Selkath to leave the Embassy. However, they refused to believe the amnesiac [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] without evidence. After Revan proved to Shasa and her friends that the Sith were corrupting them only to gain Manaan's healing agent, [[Kolto/Legends|kolto]], Shasa and the Selkath forsook the dark side and left the Embassy. Following Malak's death at a redeemed Revan's hands in the Jedi Civil War's [[Battle of Rakata Prime|final battle]], Shasa founded an [[Order of Shasa|order]] of [[Force Adept|Force adepts]] that survived for close to [[4 ABY/Legends|four thousand years]].

==Biography==
[[File:Shaelas.jpg|thumb|left|170px|Shasa's father Shaelas hired the Jedi Revan to locate Shasa during the Jedi Civil War.]]
Shasa, a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Selkath/Legends|Selkath]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], was born to [[Shaelas]] on the Selkath [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Manaan/Legends|Manaan]]. During her childhood, Shasa befriended a fellow Selkath named [[Galas (Selkath)|Galas]], who gave her a token as a gift.<ref name="KotOR game" /> In [[3956 BBY]],<ref name="KotOR CG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref> during the [[Jedi Civil War]] that was waged against the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Malak]] and his [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]], an [[Iridonian (species)|Iridonian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] was hired by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] to lure several of Manaan's youth into the [[Sith Embassy]]. Among these were Shasa and Galas.<ref name="KotOR game" />

Shasa, Galas, and several other youths joined the Sith and [[Sith training|learned]] the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] under the Embassy's [[Unidentified Dark Jedi Master (Manaan)|Dark Jedi Master]]. The Sith aimed to corrupt and brainwash the Selkath youth so that they could establish a puppet government on the planet to harvest [[Kolto/Legends|kolto]], a valuable healing agent found exclusively on Manaan.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> The Sith told Shasa and her friends that they could leave the Embassy if they wished. When Galas wanted to leave, however, they [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] him to the point of near-[[Death/Legends|death]] and deceived Shasa into believing that they had let Galas go free.<ref name="KotOR game" />

===Encountering Revan===
{{GameLSmechanics}}
[[File:Galas.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shasa's friend Galas, who, before dying, asked Revan to warn her the Sith were evil]]
{{Quote|I will give you 500 credits if can uncover the fate of my daughter and the other missing Selkath youth. Not much, but all I can afford. I have no official authority on this matter, and my personal wealth is not vast.|Shaelas, to Revan|<ref name="KotOR game" />}}
Shasa encountered the [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]—the former Dark Lord responsible for starting the Jedi Civil War—during his [[Raid on the Sith Embassy|raid]] on the Sith Embassy. Revan infiltrated the Embassy on behalf of both Shasa's [[Parent/Legends|father]], Shaelas, who was searching for Shasa, and the local Republic [[Ambassador/Legends|ambassador]], [[Roland Wann]]. Upon learning why Shasa and her friends were inside the facility, Revan attempted to convince the wayward Selkath to abandon the Sith. However, Shasa had been brainwashed by the Sith to the point of unwillingness to believe anything ill of them; even when one of her fellow apprentices mentioned the [[destruction of Taris]], Shasa stated that it was merely a Republic lie. Revan convinced Shasa and her friends to provide him the opportunity to locate actual proof of the Sith's treachery. While searching the Embassy, Revan found Galas, who gave the Jedi the token that he had given to Shasa when they were children. Before Galas died, he begged Revan to tell Shasa that the Sith were evil. Revan presented the token to Shasa, who initially refused to believe that the Sith killed Galas. After Revan convinced her to accept the truth, Shasa and her friends immediately left the Embassy and abandoned their dark side teachings.<ref name="KotOR game" />
{{Endgame}}

===Legacy===
Shortly after [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Malak's death at Revan's hands and the Sith Empire's [[Battle of Rakata Prime|defeat]],<ref name="KotOR game" /> Shasa and several other Selkath participated in a [[Ahto Uprising|battle]] against the Sith forces on Ahto City and successfully defeated the Sith.<ref name="M:DOH 3" /> Shasa realized that if Manaan was to remain completely neutral, the Selkath could not rely on the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] any more than the Sith. Nevertheless, Shasa felt that [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-sensitive Selkath needed proper training to use their [[Force power/Legends|abilities]] and avoid the dark side. Shasa and the other Selkath who received training from the Sith formed a doctrine of peace and defense, creating an organization known initially as the "[[Order of Shasa|Order]]."<ref name="KotOR CG" /> The group soon became known as the "Order of Shasa."<ref name="KotOR CG WE 7" /> Shasa's order of [[Force Adept|Force adepts]] survived her death, lasting for nearly [[4 ABY/Legends|four thousand years]]—until the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="Manaan: Depths of History">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040226manaan4|series=Manaan: Depths of History|text=Part 4: Blades of Rebellion}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
After the Sith brainwashed Shasa and the Selkath youth, the young Selkath believed everything that the Sith told her. She refused to accept anything negative toward the Sith and was convinced that the Sith were victims of Republic lies and half-truths. When she learned that the Sith had tortured and killed her childhood friend Galas, Shasa was initially reluctant to believe this revelation, but she eventually accepted the truth and apologized to Revan for doubting him.<ref name="KotOR game" /> After forming her order of Force-sensitives following the end of the Jedi Civil War, Shasa was wary of contacting the Jedi or any other Force-using organization for help. If [[darksider]]s started to advance in her order's ranks or influence new members, Shasa sought advice from a Jedi whom she trusted. Alternatively, Shasa would seek advice from a Force-based tradition with what she perceived as a good reputation, such as the [[Jal Shey]].<ref name="KotOR CG" />

Shasa was bilingual and was able to speak and comprehend [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]] and the [[Selkath (language)|Selkath]].<ref name="KotOR CG WE 7" />

==Equipment==
When she confronted Revan in the Sith Embassy, Shasa employed a [[Mandalorian heavy blaster]] and a [[Sith energy shield]] during combat. She also owned a [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic]] implant called the [[Bavakar cardio package]].<ref name="KotOR game" /> After the Jedi Civil War, Shasa possessed a [[Fira]] [[Sword/Legends|sword]], a [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]], and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="KotOR CG WE 7" /> Because she was a Selkath, she was able to breathe both [[Water/Legends|underwater]] and on land.<ref name="KotOR CG" />

==Powers and abilities==
[[File:Order_of_Shasa.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Shasa fights a Sith trooper during the Ahto Uprising, after the Jedi Civil War's end.]]
Because Shasa had a natural ability with the Force, she was capable of [[Telekinesis/Legends|gripping and moving]] objects with the Force. She also utilized the Force to perform [[Mind trick/Legends|mind tricks]] and was able to create a whirlwind of Force energy that could encircle her opponent.<ref name="KotOR CG WE 7" /> After she formed her order of Force adepts, Shasa developed other Force powers, including a technique that allowed her to sense deception with the Force. She created two other techniques, one involved using the Force to sense the [[Progenitor's Call|call]] of a giant [[Firaxan shark/Legends|Firaxan shark]] named the [[Progenitor]]—whom the Selkath believed to be their evolutionary ancestor<ref name="KotOR game" />—and wield it against her enemies. The other was a [[Waveform|telepathic power]] that imitated wave action that was similar to Manaan's oceans.<ref name="KotOR CG" /> Shasa was capable of fighting unarmed, with a blaster, and with a fira.<ref name="KotOR CG WE 7" />

==Behind the scenes==
Shasa was first featured<ref name="KotOR game" /> in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', created by [[BioWare]].<ref name="Game">{{WebCite|url=http://www.ign.com/games/star-wars-knights-of-the-old-republic/pc-16512|text=Star Wars Knights of the Old Republic|archivedate=20161027055618}}</ref> Her story was indirectly expanded by [[Cory J. Herndon]] in the third part of the [[Planet Hoppers]] article ''[[Manaan: Depths of History]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] in [[February]] [[2004]].<ref name="M:DOH 3" /> Shasa was also mentioned in the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' released on [[August 19]], [[2008]]<ref name="KotOR CG" /> and was the main subject of the seventh web enhancement for the campaign guide.<ref name="KotOR CG WE 7" /> [[Abel G. Peña]], one of the writers for the ''Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide'', was asked on his official thread on [[StarWars.com]] about an [[:File:Order of Shasa.jpg|image]] from the campaign guide depicting a Selkath fighting a [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith trooper]]. Peña confirmed that the Selkath in the image was Shasa.<ref>{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=243200&start=600|thread=The Official Abel G. Peña Thread (Halagad Approved)|user=[[Abel G. Peña]]|date=2009-10-17|nobackup=1}}</ref>

In-game, there are two ways to convince Shasa and her fellow Selkath to abandon the Sith. The first is a datapad from the Dark Jedi Master's chambers; after the player shows Shasa the datapad, she and her friends immediately depart the Embassy in order to alert the local authorities. The second is through a token given to Revan from Shasa's dying friend Galas; however, Shasa will at first become reluctant, going as far to accuse Revan of killing Galas. If the player has enough skill with persuasion or has the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Bastila Shan]] in their party, Shasa and her fellow Selkath will accept the truth.<ref name="KotOR game" /> In the non-[[canon]] dark-sided storyline, Revan can either kill Shasa and her friends or congratulate them for learning the Sith ways, allowing them to continue under the Sith's tutelage.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> Should the player fail to obtain the quest to find Shasa before entering the Sith Embassy, the player will be unable to have information to reason with the Council in Ahto City. This would result in Revan's execution upon exiting the Sith Embassy.<ref name="KotOR game" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}}
